enriched_points.expanded_content: Smart materials can dynamically respond to environmental changes, allowing systems to adapt and improve performance across various conditions, enhancing overall efficiency.
Unlike traditional actuators, smart materials are more energy-efficient, requiring power only when necessary, and feature fewer moving parts, reducing maintenance and increasing reliability.
The versatility of smart materials opens avenues for innovation in sectors like aerospace, healthcare, and robotics, fostering advancements in design and functionality of products.
